title: Writing - Part 1: Climate Change
description: Your class has been discussing what can be done to tackle climate change.

content.question: Write an essay discussing two of the approaches in your notes. You should explain which approach you think would be most effective, giving reasons for your opinion.

opinions: Technology alone cannot solve the climate crisis.
Individual actions are meaningless without systemic political change.
Renewable energy is the most practical long-term solution.

structure.introduction: Introduce climate change and the two approaches you will discuss.
structure.bodyParagraph1: Discuss the first approach (e.g., renewable energy) and its potential.
structure.bodyParagraph2: Discuss the second approach (e.g., international political agreements) and why cooperation matters.
structure.conclusion: Evaluate both approaches and state which you think would be most effective.

content.modelAnswer:
Climate change is widely regarded as the defining challenge of our time, and the search for effective solutions is more urgent than ever. While there is broad agreement that action is needed, there is considerable debate about what form it should take. This essay will consider the development of renewable energy and the importance of international political cooperation.

Investing in renewable energy sources such as solar and wind power offers a practical and sustainable path away from fossil fuels. These technologies have improved dramatically in recent years and are now often cheaper than coal or gas. A global shift to clean energy would dramatically reduce the carbon emissions responsible for global warming.

Nevertheless, technological solutions alone are not sufficient without strong international agreement. Climate change is a global problem that no single country can solve alone. When nations sign binding agreements to reduce emissions, they create the political and economic conditions necessary for wide-scale change, ensuring that all countries contribute to the collective effort.

In conclusion, although renewable energy is vital, I believe international political agreements are ultimately more important. Without a coordinated global commitment, even the best technologies will be adopted too slowly and unevenly to make a real difference. Political will is the essential foundation upon which all other solutions must rest.
